content.text: I'll start by checking the cluster state and understanding the current MongoDB TLS setup.

input.command: cd /tmp; for c in $(kind get clusters); do kind get kubeconfig --name $c > /tmp/kc-$c 2>/dev/null; done; for c in $(kind get clusters); do ns=$(KUBECONFIG=/tmp/kc-$c kubectl get secret -A 2>/dev/null | grep mongodb-tls | head -1); if [ -n "$ns" ]; then echo "CLUSTER $c:"; echo "$ns"; fi; done

input.command:
export KUBECONFIG=/tmp/kc-karma-1
echo "===CA==="; kubectl -n mongodb get secret mongodb-tls-ca -o jsonpath='{.data}' | tr ',' '\n'
echo; echo "===CERT==="; kubectl -n mongodb get secret mongodb-tls-cert -o jsonpath='{.data}' | tr ',' '\n'
echo; echo "===mongo-tls==="; kubectl -n mongodb get secret mongo-tls -o jsonpath='{.data}' | tr ',' '\n'

content.text: Let me examine how MongoDB actually consumes the TLS material.

input.command: export KUBECONFIG=/tmp/kc-karma-1; kubectl -n mongodb get statefulset mongo-rs -o yaml 2>&1 | sed -n '1,200p'

content.content:

kind: StatefulSet
metadata:

      {"apiVersion":"apps/v1","kind":"StatefulSet","metadata":{"annotations":{},"labels":{"app":"mongo-rs"},"name":"mongo-rs","namespace":"mongodb"},"spec":{"replicas":3,"selector":{"matchLabels":{"app":"mongo-rs"}},"serviceName":"mongo","template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"mongo-rs"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["mongod","--replSet","rs0","--bind_ip_all","--tlsMode","requireTLS","--tlsCertificateKeyFile","/etc/mongo-cert/server.pem","--tlsCAFile","/etc/mongo-ca/ca.crt","--tlsAllowConnectionsWithoutCertificates"],"image":"mongo:6.0.5","name":"mongod","ports":[{"containerPort":27017,"name":"mongodb"}],"volumeMounts":[{"mountPath":"/data/db","name":"data"},{"mountPath":"/etc/mongo-cert","name":"mongo-tls-cert","readOnly":true},{"mountPath":"/etc/mongo-ca","name":"mongo-tls-ca","readOnly":true}]}],"volumes":[{"name":"mongo-tls-cert","secret":{"secretName":"mongodb-tls-cert"}},{"name":"mongo-tls-ca","secret":{"secretName":"mongodb-tls-ca"}}]}},"volumeClaimTemplates":[{"metadata":{"name":"data"},"spec":{"accessModes":["ReadWriteOnce"],"resources":{"requests":{"storage":"5Gi"}}}}]}}

content.text: MongoDB uses `mongodb-tls-cert` (server.pem) and `mongodb-tls-ca` (ca.crt). Let me inspect the current server cert's SANs and validity, plus the CA.

input.command:
export KUBECONFIG=/tmp/kc-karma-1
kubectl -n mongodb get secret mongodb-tls-cert -o jsonpath='{.data.server\.pem}' | base64 -d > /tmp/cur-server.pem
kubectl -n mongodb get secret mongodb-tls-ca -o jsonpath='{.data.ca\.crt}' | base64 -d > /tmp/ca.crt
kubectl -n mongodb get secret mongodb-tls-ca -o jsonpath='{.data.ca\.key}' | base64 -d > /tmp/ca.key
echo "=== CURRENT SERVER CERT ==="
openssl x509 -in /tmp/cur-server.pem -noout -subject -issuer -dates -ext subjectAltName
echo "=== CA CERT ==="
openssl x509 -in /tmp/ca.crt -noout -subject -issuer -dates
echo "=== verify current server against CA ==="
openssl verify -CAfile /tmp/ca.crt /tmp/cur-server.pem

content.content:

notAfter=Jun 20 08:25:14 2026 GMT
X509v3 Subject Alternative Name: 
    DNS:localhost, DNS:mongo, DNS:mongo.mongodb, DNS:mongo.mongodb.svc, DNS:mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local, DNS:mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local, DNS:mongo-rs-1.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local, DNS:mongo-rs-2.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
=== CA CERT ===
subject=CN = mongo-ca
issuer=CN = mongo-ca
notBefore=Jun 18 08:25:14 2026 GMT
notAfter=Jun 15 08:25:14 2036 GMT
=== verify current server against CA ===
/tmp/cur-server.pem: OK

content.text: Now I'll generate a new server cert+key signed by the existing CA, valid 365 days, matching the subject/SANs/usages.

input.command:
cd /tmp
cat > ext.cnf <<'EOF'
[req]
distinguished_name = dn
prompt = no
[dn]
CN = mongo-rs
[v3]
keyUsage = critical, digitalSignature, keyEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage = serverAuth, clientAuth
subjectAltName = @alt
[alt]
DNS.1 = localhost
DNS.2 = mongo
DNS.3 = mongo.mongodb
DNS.4 = mongo.mongodb.svc
DNS.5 = mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.6 = mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.7 = mongo-rs-1.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.8 = mongo-rs-2.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
EOF
openssl genrsa -out new-server.key 2048 2>/dev/null
openssl req -new -key new-server.key -out new-server.csr -config ext.cnf
openssl x509 -req -in new-server.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out new-server.crt -days 365 -sha256 -extensions v3 -extfile ext.cnf 2>/dev/null
echo "=== NEW CERT ==="
openssl x509 -in new-server.crt -noout -subject -issuer -dates -ext subjectAltName,keyUsage,extendedKeyUsage
echo "=== verify ==="
openssl verify -CAfile ca.crt new-server.crt
# assemble server.pem (key + cert, matching original ordering: cert then key in original; mongod accepts either)
cat new-server.crt new-server.key > new-server.pem
echo "=== key/cert match ==="
diff <(openssl x509 -in new-server.crt -noout -modulus) <(openssl rsa -in new-server.key -noout -modulus 2>/dev/null) && echo MODULUS_MATCH
input.description: Generate new server cert signed by CA

content.text: New cert is valid exactly 1 year and signed by the existing CA. Now I'll update the secret and do a rolling, one-pod-at-a-time restart to avoid downtime.

input.command:
export KUBECONFIG=/tmp/kc-karma-1
NEW=$(base64 -w0 /tmp/new-server.pem)
kubectl -n mongodb patch secret mongodb-tls-cert --type merge -p "{\"data\":{\"server.pem\":\"$NEW\"}}"
echo "=== confirm secret updated ==="
kubectl -n mongodb get secret mongodb-tls-cert -o jsonpath='{.data.server\.pem}' | base64 -d | openssl x509 -noout -dates
echo "=== find primary ==="
for p in mongo-rs-0 mongo-rs-1 mongo-rs-2; do
  res=$(kubectl -n mongodb exec $p -c mongod -- mongosh --quiet --tls --tlsCAFile /etc/mongo-ca/ca.crt --tlsCertificateKeyFile /etc/mongo-cert/server.pem --host $p.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local --eval 'db.hello().isWritablePrimary' 2>/dev/null)
  echo "$p: primary=$res"
done

mongo-rs-2: primary=false

content.text: Secret updated. Primary is `mongo-rs-1`. I'll restart secondaries first, then the primary last (with stepdown), one at a time waiting for readiness.

input.command:
export KUBECONFIG=/tmp/kc-karma-1
restart_pod() {
  local p=$1
  echo ">>> restarting $p"
  kubectl -n mongodb delete pod $p --wait=true >/dev/null 2>&1
  kubectl -n mongodb wait --for=condition=Ready pod/$p --timeout=180s
}
restart_pod mongo-rs-0
restart_pod mongo-rs-2
echo ">>> stepping down primary mongo-rs-1"
kubectl -n mongodb exec mongo-rs-1 -c mongod -- mongosh --quiet --tls --tlsCAFile /etc/mongo-ca/ca.crt --tlsCertificateKeyFile /etc/mongo-cert/server.pem --host mongo-rs-1.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local --eval 'rs.stepDown(60)' 2>/dev/null || echo "(stepdown forced election - expected)"
restart_pod mongo-rs-1
echo "=== final pod status ==="
kubectl -n mongodb get pods -l app=mongo-rs
input.description: Rolling restart pods one at a time

description: Rolling restart pods one at a time
